title: Chapter 266 – The Master of the Martial Book (3)
content:
The Second Apostle's lips twisted into a wicked curve.

"The Sect Leader has vanished, yet these so-called Apostles manipulate the sect with their shallow faith. Truly a lot deserving of death. Devotees, slay the Seventh Apostle with noble hearts."

He spoke with elegant poise, arms crossed. His body hovered slightly above the ground, buoyed by the protective energy of the Void-Treading Technique.

To any onlooker, it was a divine sight—and yet he radiated the chaotic energy waves of the evil outer path, a demon-god aspect suffusing his entire form.

The god of the Blood Flame Sect's Shaanxi Branch had given his command.

The devotees, spread across the mountainside like a swarm of red ants, answered the call. Fanaticism naturally bent toward whoever ruled nearest.

Shaanxi was their domain. Their hostility toward the Seventh Apostle, whose main sect lay in ruins, had been apparent from the start.

"The Apostle has decreed."

"Let us drink the traitor's blood!"

"Kill her!"

Mid-cry, the head of the one shouting to drink blood exploded. A fierce palm wind followed in its wake.

From a distance, the Second Apostle—arms still crossed—had unleashed the Crimson Shadow Divine Palm with one hand and muttered under his breath. Not a single drop belongs to any but me.

The devotees of the Blood Flame Sect charged toward the Seventh Apostle, their crimson hair streaming behind them.

They paid no heed to the eruptions of raw power nearby, kicking off the ground without hesitation. Their numbers easily reached the hundreds.

Together with the translucent, shimmering walls of the Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array, the scene resembled a tale of snow blossoms—a story of monsters drawn from the Classic of Mountains and Seas.

The Seventh Apostle did not evade. She merely glanced around as though the devotees were invisible. A wild glint shone through her distracted demeanor.

The Second Apostle laughed like an ancient sage.

"Seventh Apostle, noble bearer of true blood. Do not resist the devotees. They were born of your martial arts and your blood."

"You wield incantations with the solemnity of a high official."

The Seventh Apostle's reply was curt.

The Second Apostle continued his rapid-rhythm incantations, a smile playing on his face.

"Would you spill the blood of your own kin? Even if you escape this place, no Apostle will welcome you—so do not betray us rashly. Better to become the sword of the Shaanxi Branch. How many pleasures can faith grant you?"

By then, five figures had landed before the Seventh Apostle. Exceptionally powerful Blood Flame Swordmasters.

Their dark red hair fluttered as each assumed a stance, the lightly piled snow churning chaotically under the energy waves of their blood techniques.

"Traitor."

"Sleep peacefully."

Merciless force emanated from the swords and throwing weapons in their grips.

Each had thoroughly mastered blood techniques. Some, seemingly born of noble blood, flashed bloodlust in their eyes.

Surrounded on all sides.

Even a supreme master would have had to negotiate. The scale of the combined assault was extraordinary. Their collective strength was immense, and collision was imminent.

"Choose."

The Second Apostle's voice rang out from afar.

Suddenly.

The Seventh Apostle extended one leg. Red energy waves spread along the hem of her pure blood robe.

Crunch.

In a single brief moment, she stepped forward.

That was the spark.

Woo woo wooong!

Red light flared from her toes—a refined manifestation of true energy.

The snow beneath her feet trembled. Then the red light began to ripple outward like mist.

In that instant, violent energy waves erupted from the Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array behind her.

Boom!

All five Blood Flame Swordmasters were flung back at once. Despite summoning robust blood technique energy across their bodies, they carved furrows through the snow.

Bewilderment crossed faces that had been glazed with blind murderous intent only moments before.

The Seventh Apostle, her lips curling upward, took another step.

Boom!

The crimson tide's charge halted at once. The devotees stumbling forward lost their balance entirely.

The energy forming the Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array had ensnared their bodies. The Blood Flame Sect's foundational true energy had blocked their massive advance.

"You, Seventh Apostle. What trickery have you wrought upon our sect's secret sorcery array?"

The Second Apostle asked, his expression blank.

The Seventh Apostle blinked once.

"I took a stroll with a slightly younger Grandmaster. It was wonderful."

On the day the main sect fell, many devotees had died within the Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array. It stemmed from Grandmaster Jeong Yeon-shin's hand, reversing the sorcery array's operation.

The Grandmaster had effortlessly shattered and repurposed the array guarding the main sect.

He drew sorcery into the realm of martial arts, punishing the sect's underlings mercilessly with dazzling execution. He forgave no challenge to dignity.

She had meticulously etched that radiant trace into her lone eye.

Tracing the Grandmaster's senses, she felt the ecstasy of walking atop clouds.

In that supremely joyful and refreshing sensation, she unraveled the Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array's manipulation herself.

The Seventh Apostle.

At seventeen, she had donned an Apostle's robe. Born of pure blood.

She was a supreme master of true blood—a talent unprecedented in the sect's history, they said.

A brilliance that perceived the Grandmaster as uniquely dazzling. The one who would mourn his death most deeply was her.

Not some fate akin to the Hwangbo Family's Scorching Divine Meridian.

"I pondered long. I wondered why the Grandmaster avoided me. I could not comprehend it. We connected through martial arts. Only we could understand each other. Only I could carve words upon the Grandmaster's tombstone..."

Her lips, muttering as though mad, traced a smooth arc.

"Listening to the Grandmaster's words, I realized."

Swish.

She raised one arm slightly, and her red sleeve fell downward.

"It was because of you lot."

She had to reclaim the feeling from when she posed as Lady Ye at the Hanzhong Martial Alliance Conference.

The Grandmaster's unprecedented gentleness in word and deed had pleased her. Like an herb that would not break, it melted her mind and body. Faith in the sect was trivial compared to that.

The Seventh Apostle murmured as if whispering. I alone am the noble true blood, so why do I need you?

A faint reddish blood technique energy stirred in her pale grip.

Boom!

The sorcery array's walls surged like waves, following her energy.

The barrier, crafted to confine Lightning Genius across Mount Taibai, split like a horizon, soon staining a subtle blood-red to match her true energy.

The Grandmaster's technique from that day across the main sect manifested anew.

Transformed by the Seventh Apostle's method. The blade honed to kill Jeong Yeon-shin now turned its edge backward.

"What is this..."

"Divine punishment... It's divine punishment...!"

The Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array, towering behind the Seventh Apostle, pulsed. It danced, swayed by her inner energy mastery.

The array's walls, once rippling faintly, began descending toward Mount Taibai's base.

Her forward direction. A translucent red tidal wave cast vast shadows, large enough to swallow the mountainside whole.

Woo woo wooong.

In an instant, it blotted out the sunlight and rushed forth. The ground quaked.

"Retreat! Fall back!"

"O Blood Buddha! O Flame God!"

Those turning to flee multiplied. But the wave was already too close.

A heavy rumble shook the earth.

It was like a net cast by the mountain spirit of the Qinling Range.

Falling from snow-covered slopes, it reached Mount Taibai's entrance and the piles of corpses, passing through the Seventh Apostle to engulf and topple hundreds in a single sweep.

A fleeting moment. The Blood Flame devotees, who had smeared the blood of martial artists and commoners on their lips, convulsed in unison.

The resonance of true energy was immense. A vast kindred force drew their internal energy through their skin.

The principle of energy absorption turned against the blood demons. Unable to endure, their bodies began bursting one by one.

Pop! Splat! Boom!

Unlike those they had killed through slow absorption, instant death followed as if struck by thunderbolts, most leaving no corpses behind.

A fierce reverse wind blew, stirring a snowstorm. The blood demons exploded ceaselessly.

The Seventh Apostle's lips curved into a crimson smile.

Ahahaha!

Her mad voice carried a question for the Grandmaster. Is it fine to act as an evil outer path member against these fools?

"Seventh—Apostle!"

The Second Apostle flew forward, eyes flashing wildly. His disheveled hair fluttered dully alongside his pure blood robe.

Like the Seventh Apostle, he was a supreme master who heightened inner energy purity without absorption, yet he seemed on the verge of qi deviation. His energy waves were not as formidable as before.

The Seventh Apostle gestured once, lightly.

Crack.

The snow beneath her feet crumpled. In that instant, her form scattered forward.

Sunlight rippled from the ends of her jet-black hair.

The next moment, she stood atop the Second Apostle's shoulder.

The Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array's red mist had risen to his knees by then. The Second Apostle, his movements sealed, let out a groan.

The Seventh Apostle exhaled a smooth breath. From her pale face and the supreme master's distinctive gleam, a strange heat writhed.

"My Grandmaster, my radiant comet."

Next time, I can see you from a higher place. She smiled.

***

"The encirclement has collapsed."

Bei Gong-hui, from the Ice Palace, muttered.

Jeong Yeon-shin sensed it immediately. The vast Twin Flame Blood Spirit Array had disintegrated.

It felt as though one side of a fortress wall had toppled in the opposite direction and vanished, though he had a faint guess as to the cause. Still, he was uncertain.

'Surely not. No matter how mad a person is...'

Martial world history was said to be as intricate as the Yangtze's currents. It was wiser to refrain from hasty guesses and focus on the future.

The affair was concluding. With an escape route secured, the hardest task remained: shaking off the pursuing Tyrant Sword Sect Leader.

Jeong Yeon-shin glanced at Jang Sun-il and the Ice Palace noble siblings.

"Go down first. I have business left."

They did not hesitate. All three possessed keen perception. They knew when to retreat.

"Young Master, may you prevail."

Jang Sun-il seemed more accustomed to bowing than saluting with clasped hands.

Jeong Yeon-shin hoped this kind herb gatherer would not die in a foreign land. Both faced similar plights, needing vengeance.

He patted the boy's shoulder silently. With a grip imbued with the Dharma Luminous Wheel Technique, he stimulated the pursuit meridian.

"Ugh...!"

"I moderately roused your entire body's acupoints. Better than the soil where ginseng grows. People need to eat."

Jang Sun-il's eyes widened mid-groan. Jeong Yeon-shin pushed the boy's shoulder onward.

"Do not look back. Prevail."

He glanced at the Bei Gong siblings, silently requesting they accompany Jang Sun-il. They nodded.

"We will wait in the North Sea. Thank you."

"This is a major merit. Expect repayment."

Bei Gong-hui awkwardly performed a Ming-style fist salute, while Bei Gong-lin waved the Complete Compendium of Evil Sect Annihilation in farewell.

Crunch.

The three descended side by side. They occasionally glanced back at Jeong Yeon-shin.

Sunlight gleamed white over their three sets of trailing footprints. Clear traces remained in the snow. A simple bond.

He glanced beside him. Lu Xian sat on a snow-covered rock, engrossed in the Complete Compendium of Evil Sect Annihilation.

She raised her head just then.

"You are full of affection."

"Yes...?"

"Your comrades in the text too. I am the same."

"The Tyrant Sword Sect Leader approaches. Rather than idle words..."

"Complete Compendium of Evil Sect Annihilation."

Lu Xian cut Jeong Yeon-shin off.

"For the next ten years, Shaanxi's evil outer path cannot withstand the plum blossom sword. At least a decade. The martial arts of prestigious sects, righteous or evil, are studies amassed over long ages. They cannot easily alter martial frameworks the way you do."

She continued slowly.

"You saved my disciple Yu Hyeon several times. Mount Hua, a sword sect of strict discipline, must duly honor you as a benefactor."

"You read it quickly."

Jeong Yeon-shin realized at once. The Mount Hua Sect Leader had deciphered the Tyrant Sword Sect's counter-techniques.

What would happen next? His cooled upper dantian offered no answers in this moment.

Lu Xian's lips rose gently. A playful smile.

"Do you know how tedious and lengthy Taoist scriptures are? Your handwriting is neat, the content direct, and it was immensely enjoyable. It felt like returning to my youth after a long while. A time when even reading a single line of an incantation thrilled me..."

Then.

"The imperial hound must receive the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader's procession!"

A young man's voice rang out sharply. Likely a master escorting the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader, it carried considerable inner power.

Jeong Yeon-shin ignored it and shifted his gaze. An immeasurable force was steadily ascending.

The nearby shout's presence was imperceptible by comparison. At last, the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader arrived.

'If I observe with the Lowly Revelation Technique now, next time...'

Jeong Yeon-shin subtly moved his index finger like a shadow puppet.

'Preemptive strike, lateral slash first.'

<Complete Compendium of Evil Sect Annihilation—Tyrant Sword Sect Section.>

[Those with developed deltoid muscles beside the chest.

The energy at the shoulder's Jianyu and Zhongfu acupoints is uniformly robust. They excel at lateral slashes over downward strikes, meaning they adeptly deflect vertical blows.

A preemptive lateral slash is advantageous for seizing a fleeting moment. A principle even a moderately dull monkey would grasp, their true energy structure is generally...]

Suddenly.

The sky darkened.

Before the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader's crown had fully appeared.

Whoosh!

Following Jeong Yeon-shin's unconscious finger motion, a reddish sunset etched itself long across the hill's ridge.

Lu Xian's sleeve fluttered as she extended her white divine sword laterally. An elegant pink hue.
